name: Villanueva de los Castillejos, Puebla de Guzmán, Tharsis, San Bartolomé de la Torre – circular route
name_original: Villanueva de los Castillejos, Puebla de Guzmán, Tharsis, San Bartolomé de la Torre – ruta circular
region_intro: Road cycling around Villanueva De Los Castillejos offers diverse landscapes characterized by green, rolling hills and extensive pine forests. The region, located in the western part of Huelva, features varied terrain suitable for road cyclists, including areas near the Guadiana River. Elevations reach up to Sierra Abuela, providing challenging climbs and expansive views. This combination of natural features creates a compelling destination for road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Villanueva De Los Castillejos The most popular road cycling route is Villanueva de los Castillejos, Puebla de Guzmán, Tharsis, San Bartolomé de la Torre – circular route, a 41.3 miles (66.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 3 minutes to complete. This route passes through varied terrain, including areas with unique "lunar" mining landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is El Portil Beach – El Rompido Lighthouse loop from San Bartolomé de la Torre, a moderate 50.9 miles (82.0 km) path. This route offers coastal proximity and views towards the El Rompido Lighthouse. Local road cyclists also love the El Granado – Molino Zahurdón loop from Villanueva de los Castillejos, a 35.5 miles (57.2 km) trail leading through traditional villages and past historic windmills, often completed in about 2 hours 33 minutes. Road cycling around Villanueva De Los Castillejos is defined by rolling hills, pine forests, and the Guadiana River vicinity. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 350 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Villanueva De Los Castillejos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Villanueva De Los Castillejos?
A: The region offers a diverse landscape characterized by green, rolling hills and extensive pine forests. You'll find varied terrain suitable for road cyclists, including areas near the Guadiana River. For those seeking a challenge, elevations reach up to Sierra Abuela, providing rewarding climbs and expansive views. Additionally, some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991342727/villanueva-de-los-castillejos-puebla-de-guzman-tharsis-san-bartolome-de-la-torre-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villanueva de los Castillejos, Puebla de Guzmán, Tharsis, San Bartolomé de la Torre – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, pass through unique 'lunar' mining landscapes.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or less experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, among the 17 road cycling routes available, 2 are classified as easy, making them suitable for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. These routes typically feature gentler gradients and less demanding distances, allowing you to enjoy the scenery at a comfortable pace.

Q: Are there challenging routes for experienced road cyclists in the area?
A: Absolutely. For experienced road cyclists seeking a challenge, there are 3 difficult routes available. These routes often feature significant elevation gains, such as those found around Sierra Abuela, and longer distances, providing a demanding and rewarding cycling experience. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20121694" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Granado – View of the Guadiana River loop from Villanueva de los Castillejos&lt;/a&gt;, which features over 1100 meters of elevation gain.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Villanueva De Los Castillejos?
A: There are 17 road cycling routes available around Villanueva De Los Castillejos on komoot, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. These include 2 easy, 12 moderate, and 3 difficult routes.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in Villanueva De Los Castillejos?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end your ride in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991342727/villanueva-de-los-castillejos-puebla-de-guzman-tharsis-san-bartolome-de-la-torre-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villanueva de los Castillejos, Puebla de Guzmán, Tharsis, San Bartolomé de la Torre – circular route&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20121726" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Granado – Molino Zahurdón loop from Villanueva de los Castillejos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while road cycling?
A: Road cycling routes often lead you through extensive pine forests and offer views of the rolling hills. You might also encounter the Guadiana River vicinity and, from higher points like Sierra Abuela, expansive views that can reach Portugal on a clear day. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4781127" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Bajo Guadiana&lt;/a&gt;, a dedicated cycling path, also offers scenic natural beauty.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical sites along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in cultural and historical points of interest. Routes can take you through traditional white villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5335369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Granado&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4191369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cartaya&lt;/a&gt;, known for their charming squares and historic buildings. You might also pass by historic windmills, such as the Molino Zahurdón, or the 15th-century Ermita de Nuestra Señora de Piedras Albas, nestled in the beautiful Prado de Osma.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Villanueva De Los Castillejos?
A: The region's climate, typical of Andalusia, generally offers pleasant conditions for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures and less intense sun, making for comfortable rides. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during those months.

Q: Are there road cycling routes that pass through traditional villages or offer places to stop?
A: Many routes are designed to showcase the local culture and provide opportunities for stops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20121726" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Granado – Molino Zahurdón loop from Villanueva de los Castillejos&lt;/a&gt; leads through traditional villages. These charming towns often have local cafes or restaurants where you can refuel and experience the local life.

Q: Is public transport available to access road cycling routes in the area?
A: While specific public transport options directly to every route start point may vary, Villanueva de los Castillejos is a municipality with local bus services. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for connections to nearby towns that serve as starting points for some routes. For example, routes starting from San Bartolomé de la Torre might be accessible via regional transport.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a road cycling route in Villanueva De Los Castillejos?
A: When starting a road cycling route from Villanueva de los Castillejos or other nearby towns, you can typically find public parking areas within the municipality. Look for designated parking zones in the town centers or near major attractions, which often serve as convenient starting points for many routes.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling trails near Villanueva De Los Castillejos?
A: Yes, several routes offer significant distances for long-distance cyclists. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20121694" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Granado – View of the Guadiana River loop from Villanueva de los Castillejos&lt;/a&gt; covers over 100 kilometers, providing an extensive ride through varied landscapes and along the Guadiana River.
tips:
The Hermitage of Our Lady of La Peña in Puebla de Guzmán (Huelva) is one of the most emblematic sanctuaries in the Andévalo region of Huelva province, with a deep spiritual, artistic, and cultural tradition.

Location: On Cerro del Águila (Eagle Hill), about 4 km from the town center.
Origin: Probably built in the 16th century, with significant renovations in the 17th century, 1960, and 1992.
Founding legend: According to tradition, the Virgin Mary appeared to the shepherd Alonso Gómez in the 15th century, who was instructed to build a hermitage on that site.

The Hermitage is located on the so-called Cerro del Águila, about four kilometers from Puebla de Guzmán. It was probably built in the 16th century, although the most important construction works were undertaken in the 17th century.
This village has been a mining area for many centuries, in whose mines, above all, massive pyrite was extracted for the manufacture of sulfuric acid.
The Zahurdón mill is located at the end of El Santo street, in Villanueva de los Castillejos. Typical old mill and a pleasant landscape with a picnic area and a nearby park.
